---
title: "Day 3 — Types, Casting, and Truthiness"
subtitle: "What type is this, and what does converting it actually do?"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Generic examples (reading_text, label, count).
Core question: "What type is this, and what does a cast really do?"
============================================================ -->

## The three core types

<!-- TODO: str (text in quotes), float/int (numbers), bool (True/False).
     `type(x)` inspects the type without changing anything. -->

| Value | Type |
|---|---|
| `"19.8"` | `str` |
| `19.8` | `float` |
| `0`, `21` | `int` |
| `True`, `False` | `bool` |

## Casting is non-mutating and returns a NEW value

<!-- TODO: float(reading_text) produces a number but does NOT rewrite
     reading_text (still a string). Headline misconception. -->

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — `float(reading_text)` returns a new number; it does **not** overwrite `reading_text`, which is still the string `"19.8"`.
:::

## The string concatenation trap

<!-- TODO: "21.0" + "0.6" -> "21.00.6" (glued text), NOT 21.6.
     Numeric addition needs float() on BOTH operands first. -->

```{python}
#| eval: true
"21.0" + "0.6"      # -> '21.00.6'  (string concatenation, not math)
```

## Truthiness rules

<!-- TODO: small reference table. Emphasize: truthiness reads
     empty/non-empty (strings) and zero/nonzero (numbers) — NOT meaning. -->

| Expression | Result | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `bool("reviewed")` | `True` | non-empty string |
| `bool("False")` | `True` | **still** non-empty! |
| `bool("")` | `False` | empty string |
| `bool(0)` | `False` | zero |
| `bool(3)` | `True` | nonzero |

## Truthiness ≠ meaning ≠ approval

<!-- TODO: bool("False") is True; approval needs an EXACT comparison
     label == "reviewed", not bool(label). -->

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — `type(x)`, `float(x)`, `bool(x)` as bare expressions show a value/type without changing the source variable.
:::

## Show your evidence

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — *predict* value **and** type for each cast; *debug* the concatenation and truthiness traps by naming the produced value *and* the reason.
:::
